queue_item.action: Fuse Event Timing and Long Animation Frame summaries into one fail-closed, privacy-safe bounded gesture replay diagnosis.

progress.finding: The two signals should be correlated only inside the same bounded replay. A complete pair can localize dominant input, handler, or presentation delay and concurrent main-thread congestion; an incomplete pair must remain unresolved, and association must not be described as causation.
progress.verification: 6 deterministic Node.js tests passed against byte-identical source and test content before commit.
progress.privacy_boundary: The packet retains a bounded replay identifier and aggregate timing fields only. DOM targets, selectors, event names, script attribution, raw entries, page text, private chat, and health data are excluded.
progress.uncertainty: Both specifications remain draft work, target-browser support is unverified, the 1.25 dominance ratio is a project heuristic, and same-replay association does not establish causation.

queue_item.handoff: Vercel Studio should wire R-009 and R-010 into this combiner for one trusted gesture replay, bind the packet to deployed commit identity, and verify controlled input, handler, and rendering delays on target browsers.

coordination.reason: Append-only update preserves concurrent team state and the canonical queue's historical snapshot.
